results.title: Multi-agent debate can improve reasoning under constrained conditions
results.snippet: A deterministic fixture describing evidence that model debate may improve reasoning on selected tasks, while warning about task dependence.
results.excerpt: The study reports improvements in selected reasoning tasks when independent agents critique each other, but the effect is not universal.

results.title: False consensus risks in LLM-as-judge workflows
results.snippet: A deterministic counter-evidence fixture about models agreeing without independently verifying claims.
results.excerpt: The paper argues that agreement among models is not equivalent to correctness and can amplify shared biases.

queries.match: context window long context degradation lost in the middle

results.title: Long-context retrieval can degrade when relevant facts appear mid-context
results.snippet: A fixture representing research on long-context fragility and retrieval position sensitivity.
results.excerpt: The report finds that large context windows do not guarantee reliable use of all included information.

queries.match: openrouter ollama local first model provider architecture

results.title: Provider-agnostic AI architecture with local and remote models
results.snippet: A fixture explaining the benefits and limits of local-first plus provider-optional model orchestration.
results.excerpt: Local-first model routing reduces dependency and cost for simple tasks, while remote models remain useful for high-reasoning synthesis.

queries.match: system can improve idea development quality

results.title: Structured deliberation improves AI-assisted idea development
results.snippet: A fixture demonstrating how systematic multi-model deliberation raises the quality of research ideas by enforcing evidence anchors.
results.excerpt: Teams using structured multi-agent deliberation produced ideas with higher novelty scores and fewer unsupported assertions.

queries.match: multi-model critique can reveal weaknesses missed

results.title: Cross-model critique surfaces blind spots in single-reviewer workflows
results.snippet: A fixture showing that critique from diverse models catches weaknesses that individual reviewers consistently miss.
results.excerpt: Independent cross-critique sessions identified an average of 2.3 additional weaknesses per proposal compared to single-review baselines.

queries.match: evidence auditing can reduce unsupported claims

results.title: Evidence audits reduce unsupported factual claims by 40% in LLM outputs
results.snippet: A fixture demonstrating how systematic evidence audits catch unsupported claims before they enter final recommendations.
results.excerpt: Applying a structured evidence audit reduced unsupported claims from 62% to 22% in generative research summaries.

queries.match: long-horizon memory can preserve research context

results.title: Long-horizon context preservation improves research continuity across sessions
results.snippet: A fixture showing that long-horizon memory mechanisms maintain research context and reduce re-explanation overhead.
results.excerpt: Systems with structured long-horizon context reduced information loss by 65% across multi-session research tasks.

queries.match: local-first architecture can reduce cost and increase user control

results.title: Cost and control trade-offs in local-first versus cloud-only model architectures
results.snippet: A fixture analyzing the cost and control benefits of local-first deployment for research workloads.
results.excerpt: Local-first architectures reduced per-task inference cost by 70% while improving data sovereignty, at the cost of increased setup complexity.
